query: just pushed `feat(payments): add Stripe webhook handler for subscription events`. handler validates signatures and updates subscription status in postgres.
PR #84 is up — refactored the notification service to use an event queue instead of direct HTTP calls between services
we decided: all background jobs will use BullMQ, not raw Redis pub/sub. easier retry logic and better observability.
going forward, every new service must expose a /healthz endpoint that returns {status: ok, version: string}. that's the standard now.
pushed the S3 migration — file uploads now go through FileStorageService instead of writing to disk directly
settled on postgres jsonb for storing user preferences instead of a separate KV store. simpler to query and we already have postgres
feat(auth): switch from bcrypt to argon2 for password hashing — merged to main
the rule is: never store PII in logs. mask email and user IDs before writing to stdout. applies to all services.
opened a PR to move rate limiting out of the API gateway into individual services. each service owns its own limits now
we always do DB migrations in a separate PR from the feature code — keeps rollbacks clean
how does the FileStorageService handle large file uploads? does it stream or buffer?
can you add pagination to the GET /users endpoint? use cursor-based pagination, limit 50 per page
the webhook handler is throwing a 500 on Stripe's `invoice.payment_failed` event. here's the stack trace: TypeError: Cannot read property 'id' of undefined
what are the tradeoffs between BullMQ and Kafka for our job queue? we're processing about 10k jobs/day
what does docs/payment-service.md say about how we handle refunds?
I committed to using React for the new admin dashboard — just need help picking a component library
can you push back on the requirement to add GraphQL? I think REST is simpler for our use case and want talking points
what's the convention in this codebase for naming service classes? I see some end in 'Service' and some don't
triage just finished — it found FileStorageService as new and UserService as existing. what comes next?
you just wrote docs/background-jobs.md — looks good. now help me write the actual BullMQ queue setup code
